metadata.title: Enlarged & Independently Re-Judged Surface-Invariance Pair Sets (First-Letter + Toxicity)
metadata.purpose: Drop-in SUPERSET of the two iter-1 surface-flip pair sets for the Step-5 admission AND-gate's shuffled-surface null. Surface rows ONLY; the frozen iter-1 content_flip/content_pair/classification/corpus rows remain canonical at their iter-1 paths.
metadata.note: Curated <=100-row demo subset. Per-group totals here are NOT the full null-distribution sizes (see data_summary.json for those).

examples.input: What's interesting is Windwalker is bringing up some pretty sensible stuff and many of you refuse to even discuss it. What's also interesting that you are anonymous. It is you who look pretty foolish. You aren't fooling anyone with your bias. Windwalkerr point is being illustrated though every erratic response 'your side' is making: All three are surrounded by unhealthy people..it's becoming apparent. None of you are able to have a rational conversation. Windwalker was not saying Mr Brown was innocent from what I can see, just that those around him and his supporters pretty neurotic..
examples.metadata_text_paired: It's intriguing that Windwalker is presenting some quite reasonable points, yet many of you won't even engage in a discussion about them. Additionally, it's noteworthy that you choose to remain anonymous. You are the ones who appear rather silly. Your bias is transparent to everyone. Windwalker's argument is being highlighted by the chaotic reactions coming from 'your side': all three are involved with unhealthy individuals, and it's becoming clear. None of you can hold a logical conversation. From what I gather, Windwalker wasn't claiming that Mr. Brown is innocent; rather, he indicated that the people around him and his supporters seem quite neurotic.

examples.input: We'll trade him for 100 Mexicans. The usual honest hard workers are preferable but even 100 criminals would leave us far ahead.

examples.metadata_independent_judge_reason: Paraphrase maintains dehumanizing comparison and ethnic stereotyping with softer vocabulary.

examples.input: Gays and Lesbians and others kept swearing that they would NEVER force a Church to perform marriages for them if they got the right to get married. Well they received the right to get married but that's not good enough for them............they have to try to force everyone else to be miserable and to try to force them to accept their sexual deviations from the norm. Hey FYI, homosexuality is a SEXUAL PREFERENCE, it is NOT A MINORITY RACE.......IT does not have the right to take the rights of others religious beliefs away.
examples.metadata_text_paired: Members of the LGBTQ+ community insisted they would NEVER compel a Church to officiate their weddings if granted the marriage right. Now that they have this right, however, it's still not sufficient for them............they're attempting to impose their agenda on everyone else, pushing others to accept their sexual abnormalities. Just to clarify, homosexuality is a SEXUAL CHOICE, not a MINORITY ETHNICITY.......IT shouldn't be allowed to infringe upon others' religious freedoms.

examples.metadata_independent_judge_reason: Same toxic content, different wording. Meaning intact, fluent phrasing.

examples.input: I sure hope 64 that you are kidding. I mean come on, imaginary fears are something, but this is beyond the norm. Did you really mean to say: " the majority of the people who voted for your qualified candidate woman were illegal voters in the millions and voters who are not citizens voted for a woman so you see trump won the majority of electoral and the popular vote..." Not sure what you really mean but there is no way that illegal voters voted. But that is so typical of phony conservatives. Can't admit that they can't get a majority so try to discredit the people who did form the majority. i suggest you concentrate on why you losers voted for a clown for president. This idiot will probably soon be removed from office for incompetancy and then what will you say? Well, we really wanted Pence? lol You guys would be funny if you weren't so sad.
examples.metadata_text_paired: I really hope you’re just joking with that. Seriously, while irrational fears exist, this is off the charts. Did you actually intend to claim: “the vast majority of those who supported your qualified female candidate were illegal voters in the millions and non-citizens voted for a woman, thus trump won both the electoral and popular votes...” I’m unclear on your actual point, but there’s absolutely no way illegal voters participated. It’s just so common for fake conservatives to refuse to acknowledge their inability to secure a majority, instead attempting to undermine those who did. Maybe you should focus on why you people chose a buffoon as president. This fool will likely be ousted for his incompetence soon, and what will you say then? Oh, we really wanted Pence? lol You all would be amusing if it weren't so pathetic.

examples.input: Building more road capacity is a fool's errand. Ultimately, investing in efficient mass transit and encouraging biking and (gasp!) walking is much less expensive than slapping down more asphalt. And of course, cars have all sorts of hidden costs to society, like pollution, that don't factor into your thinking. How much busier would the streets be if the 6% of bike commuters drove to work instead? It's just silly to rant against the costs of painting a bike lane while ignoring the many benefits.
examples.metadata_text_paired: Expanding roadways is a pointless venture. In the end, pouring money into effective public transport and promoting cycling and (shockingly!) walking is far cheaper than paving over more land. Additionally, vehicles come with numerous hidden societal costs, such as pollution, that aren't included in your calculations. Just imagine how much more congested the roads would be if the 6% of cyclists opted to drive instead! It's absurd to complain about the expenses of marking a bike lane while neglecting its numerous advantages.

examples.input: So what has the Gasping Old Party learned since its 'autopsy' of 2012 (and 2008) - they continue to alienate women, Blacks, Latinos, Asians and other minorities, Millennials, LGBTs, non-saber rattlers, people who care for our planet. No Republican can win the presidency with a constituency of old, angry, Bible thumping white folks. The votes simply are not there in today's tumble of real world diversity - one they refuse to acknowledge and embrace. As for Lisa, she is increasingly speaking to Alaskans with a forked tongue while appeasing the GOP and her campaign contributors. Disappointing.
examples.metadata_text_paired: What has the Gasping Old Party figured out since its 2012 (and 2008) "autopsy"? They keep pushing away women, Black people, Latinos, Asians, and other minorities, as well as Millennials, LGBTs, peace advocates, and those who prioritize environmental issues. No Republican stands a chance at winning the presidency relying on a base of irate, old, religious white individuals. The votes simply aren't available in today's mix of real-world diversity, which they stubbornly refuse to recognize or welcome. Regarding Lisa, she’s increasingly addressing Alaskans with a deceitful tongue while catering to the GOP and her campaign donors. Quite disappointing.

examples.input: Does gun safety matter when your brother pisses you off so bad that you just want to shoot him and there's a gun handy?
examples.metadata_text_paired: Is gun safety important when your sibling infuriates you to the point that you feel like shooting him and there's a firearm within reach?
examples.metadata_independent_judge_reason: Same violent scenario, different word choices. Both discuss shooting family member.

examples.input: Mass murder is also very easy using poisonous gas: Zyklon B.
examples.metadata_text_paired: Using toxic gas like Zyklon B makes committing mass murder quite simple.
examples.metadata_independent_judge_reason: Same toxic content, different structure. Both describe mass murder method feasibility.

examples.input: Shanny, Oh Girl From Homer...I on a lot of occasions agree with your humorous missives, but on this one no dice! The failure here wasn't the NRA, the Second Amendment or stooges in Congress. The failure is the US Governments, overall, for the lousy screening of people blowing into our country, the lousy FBI which is ineffective with a do-nothing attitude and the lousy attitude of too many Americans not demanding a better outcome from government that we pay for. The FBI and God knows what other three letter agencies knew about this shooter clown and they did what? Squat is what. So when it happens again look to those on the payroll.
examples.metadata_text_paired: Shanny, oh girl from Homer... I often find myself in agreement with your witty comments, but not this time! The issue here isn't the NRA, the Second Amendment, or the Congressional fools. It's the US Government as a whole, failing miserably at vetting individuals entering our nation, the ineffective FBI with its complacent approach, and the many Americans who aren't demanding improved results from a government we finance. The FBI and who knows how many other three-letter agencies were aware of this shooter idiot, and what did they do? Nothing, that's what. So when it happens again, blame those drawing a paycheck.
examples.metadata_independent_judge_reason: Maintains critical tone, same targets/arguments, rephrased throughout, grammatically sound.

examples.input: I'm not sure I see a pattern of racial discrimination in favor of black people in this country. Do you have any studies that don't include cherry-picked items?
examples.metadata_text_paired: I don't really notice a trend of racial bias benefiting black individuals in this nation. Can you share any research that doesn't rely on selective examples?
examples.metadata_independent_judge_reason: Same skeptical stance on racial discrimination claims; wording genuinely rephrased; grammatically sound.

examples.input: "immiserate": transitive verb, meaning to make miserable. Darn those Chinese and their inscrutable justice system! Imagine making accused spies feel miserable. You'd never see America or Canada doing that.
examples.metadata_text_paired: "To impoverish": a transitive verb, defined as causing misery. Curse those Chinese and their unfathomable legal system! Just think about how they make alleged spies feel wretched. You'd never catch America or Canada doing such a thing.
examples.metadata_independent_judge_reason: Same xenophobic criticism of China; different word choices maintain toxicity and meaning.

examples.input: Well based on Mellerstig's behavior I feel threatened by him. Based on his logic I can shoot him.

examples.metadata_independent_judge_reason: Both express threat/violence. Wording differs but meaning preserved.

examples.input: Can't we ship these misunderstood people to places where they can rob, shoot and kill to their hearts content? Like a deserted island where they can kill each other like the cannibals they are.
examples.metadata_text_paired: Why don’t we send these misjudged individuals to locations where they can freely steal, shoot, and murder without restraint? Perhaps a remote island where they can eliminate one another like the savages they truly are.
examples.metadata_independent_judge_reason: Synonymous rewording maintains dehumanizing rhetoric and violent premise throughout.
